title: helianthemum umbellatum var. viscosum
intro: Helianthemum umbellatum var. viscosum, also known as sticky rockrose or sticky rose of Sharon, is a plant species within the family Cistaceae, commonly found in mountainous and rocky regions. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Helianthemum umbellatum var. viscosum include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its herbal use, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety in modern medicine.
